title: Cursor pagination with multi-field tuple comparison
prompt: Write `MyApp.Blog.cursor_paginate/1` over a query sorted by `(score desc, published_at desc, id desc)` — three columns with id as unique tie-breaker. Cursor is `{score, ts, id}`. The keyset predicate must correctly handle the three-level row comparison: `p.score < ^score OR (p.score == ^score AND (p.published_at < ^ts OR (p.published_at == ^ts AND p.id < ^id)))`. Must produce zero-duplicate pages. Write to `lib/my_app/blog.ex`.

must_contain: order_by: [desc: p.score, desc: p.published_at, desc: p.id]

tier_rationale: Three-level tuple comparison is extremely error-prone; one missing `AND ==` breaks determinism.
